steps.title: Express both equations using the same ratio
steps.content: Factor the second equation to isolate $\frac{x}{y}$: $$\frac{cx}{6y} = \frac{c}{6} \cdot \frac{x}{y}$$ Since the first equation tells us $\frac{x}{y} = 72$, we can substitute into the second equation: $$\frac{c}{6} \cdot 72 = 72$$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content: Divide both sides by 72: $$\frac{c}{6} = 1$$ Multiply both sides by 6: $$c = 6$$ That confirms A is correct.
